name: Tanabata (Star Festival)
traditions: Writing wishes on paper strips (tanzaku) and hanging them on bamboo; elaborate streamer decorations; parades; stargazing.
historicalBackground: Tanabata originated from the Chinese Qixi festival, celebrating the meeting of star-crossed lovers Orihime and Hikoboshi across the Milky Way.
description: Japan's romantic Star Festival, where cities are adorned with colorful streamers and wishes are hung on bamboo under the summer stars.
